rationale: The paper focuses on quantum spatial search algorithms on temporal networks, which is a distinct domain from the user's question regarding Large Language Models (LLMs) and linguistic search agents. While both involve "search" and "temporal" concepts, the mechanisms (quantum walks vs. LLM masking strategies), variables, and empirical populations are entirely unrelated, failing to meet any inclusion criteria (a-f) and falling under the "off-domain entirely" rejection rule.
